description: Gangtok, Darjeeling, Pelling, and Lachung offer a breathtaking Himalayan experience filled with nature, culture, and serenity. Enjoy Gangtok’s monasteries and city charm, Darjeeling’s tea gardens and sunrise views, Pelling’s stunning waterfalls and mountain panoramas, and Lachung’s snowy beauty and the iconic Yumthang Valley—together creating a refreshing and unforgettable North East getaway.
cost_includes:
3 star premium hotel stay 
 All transfers and sightseeing by AC Vehicle. 
 Breakfast, Dinner,Lunch   
 Toll Tax. 
 Parking. 
 Driver Allowance. 
 Travel Goodies 
 24 x 7 On tour Assistance 
 All cost from EX Destination
cost_excludes:
Any meals other than those mentioned above inclusion 
 Airfare / Train Ticket 
 GST 5% 
 All monument entry Fees  
 Any portage at airports and hotels, tips, insurance, wine, mineral water, telephone charges, and all  
Items of personal nature. 
 Any services not specifically mentioned in the inclusions. 
 Entrance fees to Monuments & other sight-seeing points. 
 Expenses caused by factors beyond our control like rail and flight delays, roadblocks, vehicle malfunctions, political disturbances etc.                    
Anything Not mentioned in inclusions.

faqs.title: 1. What is the best time to visit Gangtok, Darjeeling, Pelling, and Lachung?
faqs.content: The best time is March to June and September to December, when the weather is pleasant and views are clear.

faqs.title: 2. How many days are ideal for this trip?
faqs.content: A comfortable trip usually requires 7 to 8 days to explore all four destinations smoothly.

faqs.title: 3. What are the must-see attractions during this tour?
faqs.content:
Gangtok: MG Marg, Tsomgo Lake, Baba Mandir

Darjeeling: Tiger Hill sunrise, Batasia Loop, Tea Gardens

Pelling: Skywalk, Khecheopalri Lake, Rabdentse Ruins

Lachung: Yumthang Valley, Zero Point (weather-permitting)

faqs.title: 4. Is there any permit required for this trip?
faqs.content: Yes. Tsomgo Lake, Baba Mandir, and Lachung/Yumthang require special permits. Your travel operator usually arranges them.

faqs.title: 5. What type of clothing should I carry?
faqs.content: Carry warm clothes, thermals, jackets, gloves, especially for Lachung, as it gets very cold. Comfortable walking shoes are also recommended.
